exemplar.title: To what extent has the implementation of the Wei Wei Irrigation Scheme improved food security in Sigor, West Pokot County, Kenya, between 2019 and 2024?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the implementation of the Wei Wei Irrigation Scheme improved food security in Sigor, West Pokot County, Kenya, between 2019 and 2024?

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and sustained purpose throughout the introduction and section headings
Concise, measurable and narrowly focused research question explicitly linked to key variables
Methodology shows awareness of mixed methods and relevant data sources
weaknesses: Minor language errors (âpastorolimsâ, âaccesâ) somewhat reduce polish
Sample size justification and stratified sampling details are thin
Limited detail on statistical controls and full model specification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation consistently communicates its geographical and temporal focus, and the research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused, and measurable, aligning with the 5-6 band descriptors. Methodological design demonstrates a coherent mixed-methods approach with relevant primary and secondary data, though some crucial details (sample representativeness, model controls, strata definitions) are only partially addressed. On balance, the sustained clarity and effective focus merit a 5 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates clear understanding of food-security theories and applies quantitative evidence accurately
Uses specialist terminology (e.g., Spearmanâs rank, stratified random sampling) generally appropriately
Interprets crop-yield statistics and household spending data with reasonable accuracy
weaknesses: Depth of theoretical discussion is limited and some claims lack source support
Frequent typos, inconsistent notation and incomplete citations impede clarity
Statistical calculations contain errors and some data tables lack measures of dispersion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows clear subject knowledge and generally effective use of sources, situating local findings within established theoretical frameworks, placing it in the 3-4 band. Terminology is mostly accurate but inconsistent formatting and technical errors prevent a top-band award. A score of 4 reflects solid knowledge and use of concepts with room for improved precision and depth.

strengths: Data application is directly relevant to the research question, with clear linkage between findings and hypotheses
Logical progression from evidence presentation to argument supports coherent discussion
Acknowledgement of limitations demonstrates critical reflection
weaknesses: Comparative analysis lacks depth in examining confounding factors and causality
Statistical significance tests are absent and some numerical inconsistencies remain
Argument contains minor inconsistencies (sample size switching, undefined categories)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies research data with clear relevance (mid-band), performs generally good analysis with supported conclusions, and develops an effective argument with minor inconsistencies, fitting the 7-9 band. While not yet âexcellent and consistentlyâ critical, the overall quality of application, evaluation and discussion warrants an 8 out of 12, recognising both strengths and areas for deeper analytical rigor.

strengths: Structure aligns closely with expected extended essay format, guiding the reader effectively
Well-detailed table of contents and clear section numbering support navigation
Word count respected and consistent pagination aids professionalism
weaknesses: Layout shows occasional formatting glitches (LaTeX remnants, misaligned tables)
Duplicate figure numbering and inconsistent heading styles detract from presentation polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs organization and structure clearly support understanding and meet the top-band descriptor for Presentation. Although layout and formatting errors are present, they do not significantly impair comprehension. Awarding 4 out of 4 reflects a very strong structural framework with minor cosmetic issues.

strandSelections.reasoning: Primary and secondary evidence is applied directly to the research question: tables compare pre- and post-scheme yields, correlation links the scheme to household spending, and survey graphics connect to food-security perceptions.  The relevance of each dataset is explicit; however, sample size is small and some secondary references are descriptive.  Thus application is good with clear relevance but not âexcellent and consistentlyâ so, warranting a mid-band score of 5.

The student outlines primary surveys/interviews (n = 16-17), secondary sources, crop-yield statistics and correlation tests, showing an awareness of mixed methods and sampling strategies.  Nevertheless, details are thin (no questionnaire in main text, limited justification of sample size, limited explanation of statistical treatment beyond Spearmanâs r).  Source range is modest and reliability issues are only briefly acknowledged.  Hence the methodology is âmostly completeâ but not fully âappropriate and comprehensiveâ, aligning with the 3-4 band.

The essay demonstrates understanding of food-security theories (Entitlement, FAD, Water-Food-Energy nexus) and applies them to the local context.  Quantitative evidence on yields and household spending is interpreted accurately.  However, depth is limited: theoretical discussion is brief, citations sometimes generic, and some claims (e.g., conflict reduction) are unsupported.  Overall, knowledge is clear with partially effective use of sources, fitting the 3-4 band and meriting the upper end.

Pagination, maps, photographs and tables are present; titles and figure numbers are mostly supplied.  The work respects word limit, includes a word count, and employs a readable font.  Formatting errors (occasional LaTeX remnants, mis-aligned tables, mathematical notation glitches) detract from professional appearance, but basic layout conventions are met and support communication, positioning the work in the lower half of the 3-4 band.

Specialist terms such as âSpearmanâs rank correlationâ, âsemi-aridâ, âfood availability declineâ and âstratified random samplingâ are used and generally defined correctly.  Nevertheless, there are repeated spelling and syntax errors, occasional misuse of mathematical notation, and inconsistent italicisation of theoretical names, so accuracy is not fully consistent.  This places the work solidly in the 3-4 band but closer to its midpoint.

The student states the geographical theme, spatial frame and temporal frame early, and the introduction repeatedly links the investigation to food security in Sigor.  The purpose of the study is evident throughout the table of contents and section headings, so the reader never loses sight of the focus.  Minor language problems (âpastorolimsâ, âaccesâ) and some digressions reduce polish, yet overall clarity, purpose and focus are sustained, meeting the descriptor of the 5-6 band.

A line of argument progresses from hypotheses through data presentation to conclusions, generally logical and easy to follow.  Minor inconsistencies appear (switching sample sizes, occasional circular reasoning) and some assertions outpace evidence.  Nevertheless, the discussion links back to hypotheses and theory, giving an effective if imperfect argument.  This fits the descriptor âeffective with minor inconsistenciesâ, justifying a score of 4 within the 4-6 band.

The student undertakes basic comparative analysis, calculates Spearmanâs r and comments on statistical meaning.  Tables and pie charts are interpreted, and limitations are acknowledged.  Yet the evaluation of causality, confounding factors and robustness is thin; numerical results are not tested for significance, and conclusions sometimes over-generalise from small samples.  Hence analysis is âadequate/good with mostly supported conclusionsâ, aligning with the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The essay follows a clear EE structure: abstract elements, contents page, sequentially numbered sections, conclusion, evaluation, references and appendices.  Headings and sub-headings guide the reader and support understanding.  Although some sub-section numbering is inconsistent and theoretical framework placement is unconventional, the overall structure is appropriate and aids comprehension, satisfying the top band for Presentation.

The research question is explicitly worded, narrow in space and time, and measurable (âto what extentâ¦ between 2019 and 2024â).  It links directly to an identifiable development project, allowing data collection and evaluation.  Because the wording already embeds the key variables (implementation and food security) it is clearly focused and addresses the research issue effectively, matching the top-band characteristics.

annotations.comment: Figures 2 and 3 use duplicate numbering and captions; renumber sequentially to avoid confusion.
Image captions omit publication year and full source details; provide complete citations for credibility.
The theoretical framework cites secondary summaries (ResearchGate) rather than original sources; reference Sen (1981) directly for the Entitlement Theory.
The three theoretical frameworks are described in isolation; integrate and critically compare their predictions to strengthen analysis.
The sample size and sampling approach differ between sections (n=17 vs n=16); clarify and maintain consistency.
Categories like âgreatly increasedâ vs âsomewhat increasedâ lack operational definitions; define thresholds for clarity.
Despite H3 predicting some decline, no respondents reported worsened food security; discuss possible reasons or anomalies.
The title is prominently placed at the start, clearly stating the investigationâs focus and meeting the requirement for a title at the reportâs start.
The word count of 3979 is clearly shown and stays within the 4000-word limit, satisfying the formatting requirement.
The research question is concise, measurable and narrowly focused on Wei Weiâs impact between 2019 and 2024, addressing the research issue effectively.
Session and subheading formatting vary in style; consider applying consistent heading levels, font sizes and spacing throughout the introduction section.
The table of contents is well-structured and detailed, facilitating navigation and reflecting a logical report flow.
Section numbering is inconsistent (e.g., two entries numbered â1.6â); revise numbering to maintain a clear hierarchical structure.
Numerous typos (e.g., âWorld Blankâ instead of âWorld Bankâ) and run-on sentences impede clarity; proofread and correct source citations.
The introduction would benefit from breaking into shorter paragraphs and explicitly linking statistics to hypotheses for stronger cohesion.
Hypothesis 3 contains a subjectâverb agreement error and a spelling mistake (âhindered the effectivenesâ); revise to âhave hindered the effectiveness.â
The aim statement is wordy and repeats the research question; tighten it to focus on the key variables and the method of assessment.
Fig. 1 is clearly captioned and sourced, effectively situating the study area for spatial context.
The map lacks a scale bar or north arrow; include these elements to enhance spatial accuracy and interpretation.
Sample size (n=17) is stated without justification of statistical representativeness; explain how this number ensures valid inference.
The methodology mentions controlling for rainfall variability and soil type but lacks detail on statistical controls or model specifications.
Stratified random sampling is noted but the strata definitions, sizes and rationale are not described; add these details to confirm methodological rigor.
The crop yield table uses ââ¤â and ââ¥â instead of actual averages and measures of dispersion; present precise values and error margins.
The Spearmanâs rank table shows Î£dÂ² = 26 but the formula uses Î£dÂ² = 12; correct this inconsistency to ensure accurate correlation.
The calculation of râ uses an incorrect Î£dÂ² value; recompute using the correct sum of squared rank differences for validity.
The evaluation describes âvolunteersâ but earlier cited random sampling; reconcile terminology to avoid confusion.
Phrases like âfixed investigationâ are awkward and obscure meaning; rewrite for clarity and precision.
Reference list entries show inconsistent formatting and missing retrieval dates; adopt a uniform citation style and complete all fields.
The KVDA reference lacks a clear retrieval date and URL formatting; add full web address and access date for completeness.
Job categories (âsome additional jobsâ vs âsignificantly more jobsâ) overlap; provide clearer category definitions or quantitative ranges.
The conclusion summarizes findings but omits reflection on methodological limitations; include a concise evaluation in the final section.
